chunk_summary: This section outlines a structured discharge nutrition checklist for preterm and low birth weight infants, emphasizing growth targets, feeding plans, micronutrient prescriptions, and follow-up monitoring protocols.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of a written nutrition bundle for preterm infant discharge planning.
Learn target growth trajectories and weight gain goals for very preterm infants (e.g., 15–20 g/kg/day).
Identify feeding types, fortification plans, and micronutrient prescriptions for preterm infants.
Recognize follow-up protocols and monitoring strategies for growth and metabolic bone disease.
key_facts: Target weight gain for very preterm infants is typically 15–20 g/kg/day, with some units using 10–15 g/kg/day.
Feeding plans may include mother’s milk, fortified human milk, preterm formula, or combinations.
Micronutrient prescriptions include iron (mg/kg/day based on birth weight and corrected age) and vitamin D (dose per unit protocol).
Monitoring for metabolic bone disease involves biochemical screening (e.g., alkaline phosphatase, serum phosphorus).
Follow-up should use corrected age for growth assessment, with weekly reviews initially, then spaced once stable.
reference_quote: Many neonatal references use a weight gain goal around 15–20 g/kg/day for very preterm infants, while some unit frameworks use 10–15 g/kg/day; specify the local target and the action threshold when gain falls below it.
document_key_topics: Growth targets and weight gain goals for preterm infants
Micronutrient supplementation and metabolic bone disease monitoring
Follow-up protocols and referral pathways for post-discharge care
